title: Maximum Value Sum by Placing Three Rooks I
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: C folder with the minimum-memory champion. Recommended and fastest-runtime proposals are implemented in C++; see crossReferences.

variants.languageReason: C has the smallest practical LeetCode runtime baseline among the allowed languages and the algorithm uses only scalar top-three buffers with direct int** board access.

languageAlternatives.assessment: Can implement the same constant-auxiliary algorithm, but vector wrapper and C++ runtime baseline are slightly larger than C for a memory-only objective.
Selected: direct pointer access, no heap allocation, no object metadata, and minimal runtime baseline.
Native and safe, but wrapper overhead and bounds-check ergonomics are heavier for this tiny scalar state.
Runtime, GC, and slice metadata make it less memory-lean than C.
Managed runtime and object metadata dominate the auxiliary memory saved by the algorithm.
VM and object representation overhead are not competitive for a minimum-memory target.

variants.approachJustification: For each triple on the smaller dimension, rescan the opposite dimension and keep only local top-three candidate buffers before checking 27 placements.

reasoningSummary: The memory variant relies on the same top-three correctness observation but recomputes candidates for each line triple to avoid retaining any table.
